quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure — '63.1% of all units', 'roughly 35% below the North', plus explicit revenue/unit figures for each product and region.

specificity.why: Every recommendation names a product, region, and figure (e.g. 'Push Gadget in the West, where its single order earned $299.00' and 'Review Sprocket's $4.75 price').

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations, notably that Sprocket 'drives 63.1% of units but the lowest product revenue' and Gadget's high revenue 'from only 16 units sold'.

quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. "Sprocket moved 238 units, 63.1% of all units, yet earned just $1,130.50" and "West trails at $1,414.82, roughly 35% below the North."

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product, region, and figure, e.g. "Push Gadget in the West, where its single order earned $299.00" and "Review Sprocket's $4.75 price."

insight_density.why: It surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations such as the price/volume inversion ("Sprocket... drives 63.1% of units but the lowest product revenue") and Gadget's high revenue from only 16 units.

quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g. 'Sprocket moved 238 units, 63.1% of all units, yet earned just $1,130.50' and 'West trails at $1,414.82, roughly 35% below the North.'
